confirmed.summary: Spec/plan misdiagnose why the sonarcloud job never runs on PRs and mislabel the analysis as merge-triggered 'post-merge'
confirmed.evidence: ci-quality.yml:2837 gates the whole job on `if: always() && (github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')` — it runs on neither pull_request nor push. SC-001a (spec.md:67) attributes non-execution to 'Fork PRs receive no SONAR_TOKEN, so the sonarcloud job's analysis does not run pre-merge' — wrong cause: even a same-repo token-bearing PR won't run it because of the event gate. SC-001b (spec.md:68) frames verification 'after the first token-bearing analysis on main post-merge', but push to main is excluded (job comment ci-quality.yml:2790: 'pushes skip it'), so the first analysis is the nightly cron (ci-quality.yml:68, '17 2 * * *') or a manual dispatch.
confirmed.recommendation: Correct the causal claim (event-type gate, not token) and rewrite SC-001b's verification window: after merge, the projectVersion only takes effect on the next scheduled nightly run or a manual `workflow_dispatch` of ci-quality.yml — an operator checking immediately post-merge will see no change. State this so the config-assertion (SC-001a) is designed to parse the workflow YAML statically, not to observe the job running in a PR.

verdict.reasoning:
All load-bearing evidence verified against live artifacts. ci-quality.yml:2837 is the JOB-LEVEL if for the sole `sonarcloud` job and reads exactly `if: always() && (github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')` — confirmed by indentation context (2792 job / 2793 runs-on / 2794 needs / 2837 if / 2839 steps). No other sonar job or PR-triggered sonar path exists in any workflow. Therefore the sonarcloud analysis runs on neither pull_request nor push; the token step (2840) is never reached on a PR.

Claim 1 (SC-001a misdiagnosis) is real: spec.md:67 says 'Fork PRs receive no SONAR_TOKEN, so the sonarcloud job's analysis does not run pre-merge.' The causal 'so' is wrong — the controlling mechanism is the event-type gate, not the token. A same-repo token-bearing PR would still not run the job. The author borrowed the fork/no-secrets model that applies to OTHER jobs (IS_FORK_PR at line 3311), not to sonarcloud. The plan repeats the same mistaken framing at plan.md:29.

Claim 2 (SC-001b timing) is real: the job comment ci-quality.yml:2790 ('pushes skip it') matches the 2837 gate — merge/push to main does not trigger the analysis. spec.md:68 frames it as 'the first token-bearing analysis on main post-merge' without stating that this analysis is actually the nightly cron (ci-quality.yml:68, '17 2 * * *') or a manual workflow_dispatch, so an operator checking immediately post-merge sees no change. That window is undocumented.

Not stale (all line numbers match), not misread, and not already-handled: grep of spec.md/plan.md for schedule|workflow_dispatch|cron|nightly|event_name returns nothing.

Adversarial refutation attempts fail: the parenthetical's parts are individually true but the causal claim is false; SC-001b is careful about the PR/post-merge axis but silent on the merge-does-not-trigger-analysis dependency. The one mitigant is that the actual deliverable (SC-001a static YAML-parse config-assertion) is correctly designed and unaffected by the wrong cause — which is why this is not high. But the misdiagnosis corrupts the mission's own success-verification procedure (SC-001b, how #2421 is confirmed) and would mislead a post-merge operator (up to ~24h wait or manual dispatch). Medium retained: real spec/plan drift with a concrete operator-confusion failure mode, but no functional/deliverable breakage.

confirmed.summary: SC-001b says the projectVersion/baseline reset is 'confirmed via the tool', but the tool's specced subcommands cannot query projectVersion or analyses
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:68 SC-001b '...confirmed via the tool against the public read API' and plan.md:29 verify; yet FR-005 (spec.md:43) and IC-03 (plan.md:36) give the tool only quality-gate status / coverage metrics / per-file uncovered lines / issues-by-rule/file — none read projectVersion or /api/project_analyses. Notably plan.md:12 lists /api/project_analyses/search as a dependency, but no concern/subcommand consumes it.
confirmed.recommendation: Add an analyses/projectVersion read subcommand to FR-005/IC-03 (backing the already-listed /api/project_analyses/search dependency), or reword SC-001b so it does not claim the tool confirms the baseline reset.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified against the live artifacts; the finding withstands adversarial refutation. (1) SC-001b (spec.md:68) states the real projectVersion + baseline reset is "confirmed via the tool against the public read API." (2) US3 (spec.md:25) explicitly binds "the projectVersion check (SC-001b)" to this canonical tool "instead of ad-hoc API calls." (3) Yet FR-005 (spec.md:43) and IC-03 (plan.md:36) enumerate the tool's read-only capabilities as exactly four — quality-gate status, coverage metrics, per-file uncovered lines, issues-by-rule/file — none of which reads projectVersion or an analyses list. (4) plan.md:12 lists /api/project_analyses/search as a Primary Dependency, but a full-mission grep shows it appears only on that line: no IC/subcommand/task consumes it (orphan dependency), and it is precisely the endpoint that would back a projectVersion/analyses read. So there is a genuine, multi-artifact internal contradiction. Not already handled: tasks/ is empty (only .gitkeep + boilerplate README), there is no research/ or acceptance doc, and the prior post-spec.json findings addressed a different axis (fork-PR/token testability and the move-vs-author script provenance), not the tool's inability to read projectVersion/analyses.

Steelman refutation considered: the "quality-gate status" subcommand hits /api/qualitygates/project_status, whose new-code period carries mode/date, arguably confirming the "no longer anchored at 2026-03-21" half. This partially mitigates but does not defeat the finding: (a) the spec never says the quality-gate subcommand surfaces the period baseline date, and (b) SC-001b's first clause — "reports the real projectVersion (matching pyproject.toml)" — requires reading the current analysis's version, which needs /api/project_analyses/search or /api/components/show, neither exposed by the four subcommands. The orphan-dependency fact is independently, unambiguously true regardless of any API-semantics debate. All cited line numbers are accurate.

Severity: medium (keep). It is a real coverage/consistency gap spanning US3 ↔ FR-005/IC-03 ↔ SC-001b ↔ plan.md:12 that, uncorrected, yields a tool that cannot do what US3/SC-001b say it does, forcing the ad-hoc API calls the mission set out to eliminate. Not high: SC-001b is explicitly a non-gating post-merge observation (cannot block review or cause acceptance of broken work), the baseline-date half is partially mitigable via the quality-gate period, and the fix is trivial at this pre-tasks stage. The recommendation (add an analyses/projectVersion subcommand backing the already-listed /api/project_analyses/search dependency, or reword SC-001b) is correct.

dropped.summary: FR-001's entire value rests on an unverified server-side setting (SonarCloud 'New Code' = previous_version); if it is a fixed date, wiring projectVersion is inert
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding does not withstand adversarial refutation at its stated severity (high, boundary) or framing ("FR-001's entire value rests on an unverified server-side setting; if it is a fixed date, wiring is inert; the real fix is an out-of-repo manual flip the spec fails to call out").

REFUTED PREMISE: The finding's core worry — the New Code type "might be a fixed date (2026-03-21)" — is contradicted by present repo evidence. A sibling prior mission that actually investigated this exact SonarCloud project documents the definition: kitty-specs/quality-devex-hardening-3-2-01KRJGKH/research.md:60 states "New-code window: anchored to the previous version baseline (Sonar default)" and :51 "The current 'previous version' baseline." So the definition is previous_version, exactly as the spec (spec.md:12) asserts — not a fixed date. Furthermore, that mission explicitly offered "OPTION C — reset the baseline [to a specific date]" (sonar-pre-flip-verification.txt) but recommended against it and took Option A (coverage tests), leaving the gate "informational" (mission-review.md:167). No artifact anywhere shows a baseline-to-date reset being executed. The "2026-03-21" string the finding leans on is a commonplace mission date across the repo (e.g. mission 055), not a documented Sonar baseline-config date.

MECHANISM IS CANONICAL: Under "Previous Version," a never-changing ("not provided") version freezes the leak period, and wiring sonar.projectVersion from pyproject.toml (currently 3.2.5) resets it per bump — standard SonarCloud behavior. The in-repo half is also accurate: .github/workflows/ci-quality.yml "[ENFORCED] Materialize effective Sonar config" (~L3105) and Scan args (~L3129) never emit sonar.projectVersion, confirming the "not provided" claim.

BOUNDARY IS ALREADY HANDLED: The mission does not conflate repo-change with server-side effect. spec.md:67-68 splits SC-001 into SC-001a (in-repo, CI-verifiable wiring) and SC-001b (post-merge server-side empirical observation via the tool). SC-001b is precisely an empirical catch that would surface inert wiring; it is not silently assumed. The recommendation's second half (add a "required manual step: flip the server-side definition") is predicated on the fixed-date counterfactual the evidence contradicts, and would inject a likely-false caveat.

SURVIVING KERNEL (LOW): It is literally true that this mission's spec.md/plan.md never query/pin api/new_code_periods/show pre-flight (grep confirms zero mentions; the mission's own research/ dir is empty; provenance mission 01KWV531 is absent from this checkout). But this is a modest defense-in-depth hardening — trivially absorbed by the FR-005 read-only tool being built anyway — not a high/boundary defect, and it does not justify the finding's "entire value rests on an unverified setting" framing, since the setting is verified-by-provenance and backstopped by SC-001b. Net: real=false at the asserted severity; residual value is a LOW hardening nicety.

dropped.summary: No single-authority decision for the projectVersion injection site; org/projectKey are already dual-sourced, so an 'OR' invites a silent precedence trap
dropped.why_refuted:
All raw evidence is LIVE and accurate (not stale): IC-01/plan.md:28 does contain the "OR"; Materialize appends sonar.organization/projectKey at ci-quality.yml:3119-3120; the scanner passes -Dsonar.organization/projectKey at 3135-3136; sonar.branch.name is Materialize-only at 3121; and SonarScanner -D args do override the properties file. So the finding is not stale or misread on its facts. However, its MEDIUM "dual-authority / silent precedence trap" framing does NOT withstand adversarial scrutiny, on four independent grounds:

1) No dual authority is actually planned. The plan's "OR" (and the spec's "and/or" at FR-001/SC-001a) is a menu offering the implementer ONE of two injection mechanisms, not an instruction to populate both. The plan does not create two authorities for projectVersion; it fails only to explicitly ban a hypothetical future second site.

2) SC-001a already steers to a single site. plan.md:29 / spec.md:67 require that projectVersion "appears in the printed effective sonar-project.properties." That printed file is produced only by the Materialize step; a scanner-arg-only injection would NOT write into sonar-project.properties and would fail SC-001a. So the mission's own success criterion effectively MANDATES the Materialize/properties route — which is exactly the single-site, runtime-derived-value (sonar.branch.name-style, line 3121) precedent the finding recommends. The plan already lands on the recommended outcome.

3) The org/projectKey "dual-sourced" evidence is a partial misread. Both the Materialize (3119-3120) and scanner (3135-3136) sites reference the SAME single computed output steps.sonar_settings.outputs.organization/.project_key (computed once at 2871-2908). They are benign redundancy from one authority and can never silently disagree — so they are NOT themselves a precedence trap. Citing them as proof that "OR invites a silent precedence trap" overstates the risk; a real trap needs two DIFFERENT value sources, which the plan does not introduce (FR-002 forces one pyproject source). The file's patterns are in fact mixed (host_url scanner-only at 3134; branch.name/coverage Materialize-only at 3121-3122; org/projectKey both), so no single pattern compels a future maintainer to add a second site.

4) FR-002 (spec.md:40) already enforces value single-sourcing (pyproject canonical, no duplication into committed properties), covering the substantive single-authority concern.

Residual kernel: the plan does not EXPLICITLY forbid also adding a redundant -Dsonar.projectVersion scanner arg, nor document the -D>properties precedence. That is a legitimate but low-severity, optional plan-hygiene nicety, and it is already largely pre-empted by SC-001a and FR-002. It does not rise to a medium dual-authority defect. Verdict: the finding as framed is refuted (real=false); if retained at all, it should be downgraded to low and recast as an optional one-line "single injection site" clarification, not a present precedence hazard.

dropped.summary: Load-bearing grounding (Stijn's research.md) is cited as settled but is absent from the repo/dossier, making the plan's core assumptions unauditable
dropped.why_refuted: The finding's three factual observations are correct (citations at spec.md:5/11/64, plan.md:5, meta.json; mission ci-hygiene-and-sonar-debt-remediation-01KWV531 absent from kitty-specs/; research/ dir empty). But its severity-justifying claim — that the plan's "core assumptions" are "unauditable" and "all derive from that absent artifact" — is refuted against the live repo. (1) The projectVersion/previous_version-baseline claim is directly auditable and verified TRUE: sonar-project.properties has no sonar.projectVersion, the ci-quality.yml "Materialize effective Sonar config" step (lines 3110-3127) and scanner args (3133-3136) inject none, and a case-insensitive grep for projectversion across the whole workflow returns 0 hits; spec.md:11 says this was "confirmed against the live repo" and .gates/post-spec.json corroborates independent in-mission verification by architect-alphonso + reviewer-renata (16 projectVersion mentions). (2) The untracked-script claim is auditable in-repo (git ls-files shows no tracked sonarcloud_branch_review.sh; scripts/ci/ holds only quality_gate_decision.py) and the mission is explicitly architected NOT to depend on the absent artifact — FR-005/IC-03/commit e7fbdc09f author fresh to a behavioral contract with a non-blocking hedge. (3) The token-free public-read-API point is a flagged Assumption (spec.md:79), testable at implement time, and de-risked by a recorded fixture so CI needs no network/SONAR_TOKEN (plan.md:37). Moreover the recommendation is largely already satisfied: the three reused determinations are transcribed inline in spec.md:12-14 and plan.md Key Decisions, so a reviewer can audit the assumptions from the dossier today. The residual — transcribing a cross-fork provenance artifact into research/ — is at most a low documentation-hygiene nit (and the domain tag "coverage" is a mislabel). Facts accurate, but the load-bearing "unauditable" premise does not withstand scrutiny at medium; real=false.

dropped.summary: Split-brain coverage doc: FR-004's new coverage-signals.md overlaps an existing coverage-gate doc that already owns the diff-coverage explanation
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's literal evidence is accurate (the proposed docs/guides/coverage-signals.md at plan.md:19/33/50; the pre-existing docs/plans/testing/ci-coverage-gate-tuning.md; both pointing at the same diff-cover step in ci-quality.yml; the plan naming neither), but its load-bearing framing — "split-brain," "already owns the diff-coverage explanation," "two orphaned homes rather than one discoverable authority" — is materially overstated and partly false, so it does not withstand refutation.

1) The premise that the tuning doc "owns the diff-coverage explanation" is contradicted by the doc itself. ci-coverage-gate-tuning.md explicitly disclaims authority: "See the `diff-cover` step in `.github/workflows/ci-quality.yml` for the authoritative list." It is in fact already stale against the live workflow — the doc lists 3 critical-path entries (status/, mission_detection.py, dashboard/handlers/) while the live ci-quality.yml (lines 2704-2715) now enumerates 10 (adding src/kernel/*, src/doctrine/*, src/charter/*, merge/*, runtime/next/*, mission_runtime/*, dashboard/scanner.py). The single discoverable authority for the diff-cover file set therefore already exists — the workflow — and the plan's IC-02 correctly points at that same workflow step as its source of truth. So "two orphaned homes / not one authority" is wrong: both the existing doc and the plan already converge on the workflow.

2) "Split-brain / overlap" overstates a mere adjacency. The tuning doc's subject is calibration of the INTERNAL gate (brittle-vs-lenient tuning, pragma:no-cover, when to add files) and says nothing about SonarCloud. The proposed coverage-signals.md's subject is the cross-system RECONCILIATION of SonarCloud whole-repo coverage vs the internal diff-coverage — a reviewer interpretation guide (FR-003/004) whose primary content appears nowhere in the existing doc. There is no duplicated explanation to fork; at most the new guide briefly restates the internal gate to contrast it. The two also sit in defensibly different trees/Divio types (docs/plans/testing tuning-note vs docs/guides reviewer how-to).

3) It targets a plan, not an implementation, and the plan already carries the exact remediation intent. FR-004 and IC-02's deliverable both require the doc be "referenced from the coverage/quality-gate context so a PR reviewer finds it," and the path is illustrative ("e.g. docs/guides/coverage-signals.md"). Nothing precludes cross-linking the tuning doc; FR-004's language actively invites it. Choosing that specific link target is an ordinary implementation decision under an already-present discoverability requirement, not a plan-level defect.

Residual nugget: cross-linking the related tuning doc would be a reasonable, low-cost touch and is consistent with DIR-032 drift-avoidance. That justifies at most a low/informational hint — not a "split-brain authority" defect. Because the headline claim misreads the authority relationship (the tuning doc is explicitly non-authoritative and stale, the true authority already exists and is referenced by both artifacts) and the plan already satisfies the underlying FR-004 discoverability intent, the finding does not clearly withstand adversarial scrutiny. Verdict: not real.

dropped.summary: Materialize step lacks a strip-guard for a pre-existing projectVersion, reopening the exact FR-002 'no duplicate key' hazard
dropped.why_refuted:
REFUTED. The finding's code description is factually accurate (Materialize step at ci-quality.yml:3105-3128 strips org/projectKey/coverage.reportPaths/xunit.reportPath via a grep -v chain at 3111-3114, then re-appends), but its actionable claim does not withstand scrutiny on five independent axes:

(1) CIRCULAR/SPEC-FORBIDDEN PREMISE. The hazard requires 'a later static sonar.projectVersion= added to sonar-project.properties.' That is exactly what FR-002 (spec.md:40, 'not hardcoded or duplicated into sonar-project.properties') and IC-01 (plan.md:28, 'no duplicate key in sonar-project.properties') forbid. The finding cites FR-002 as the thing being violated, but FR-002 is precisely what prevents the premise — a strip guard would defend against a future spec violation, i.e., belt-and-suspenders, not a defect.

(2) DOUBLY-HYPOTHETICAL ABOUT UNIMPLEMENTED CODE WITH A MOOTING ALTERNATIVE. projectVersion is wired nowhere yet (zero hits in yml/properties/toml; branch has only dossier changes). IC-01 offers two co-equal routes — the -Dsonar.projectVersion= scanner arg OR append-to-materialized-properties. The existing SonarCloud Scan step already passes org/projectKey as -D args, the natural route; if chosen, the strip chain is entirely irrelevant. The finding presumes the append route + omission of a guard + a later FR-002 violation.

(3) MISREADS FR-002. FR-002 concerns the version source-of-truth (single-sourced from pyproject.toml, survives a bump with no edits), not runtime grep-strip duplicate-line mechanics; the finding relabels single-sourcing as a 'no duplicate key' runtime hazard.

(4) MISCHARACTERIZES THE STRIP-CHAIN INVARIANT. sonar-project.properties statically contains org/projectKey/coverage.reportPaths/xunit.reportPath (all guarded) — the guards target keys actually present statically. But sonar.branch.name is re-appended with NO strip guard (not static in the file) and xunit.reportPath is stripped with NO re-append. So the real contract is 'strip keys that pre-exist statically,' not 'strip everything you append.' Per FR-002 projectVersion will not be static, so there is nothing to strip; 'mirror the existing defensive pattern' misapplies the pattern.

(5) ALREADY HANDLED AT PLAN LEVEL. IC-01 explicitly commits to 'no duplicate key in sonar-project.properties,' so the concern is already the plan's stated approach.

Net: accurate code description, but a speculative, spec-prohibited, already-handled defense-in-depth suggestion rather than a real defect. Severity residual is at most a trivial nicety (low), already covered by the FR-002/IC-01 commitment; under the default-to-false bar it does not withstand.

dropped.summary: SC-001b/SC-001a describe a post-merge/token trigger that does not exist: the sonarcloud job runs ONLY on schedule/workflow_dispatch, never on push or PR.
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's CI facts are accurate: ci-quality.yml:2837 gates the sonarcloud job on `schedule || workflow_dispatch` only, the workflow's own comment (:2788-2790) states "PRs skip Sonar entirely... and pushes skip it," and the SONAR_TOKEN check (:2840-2862) is a secondary inner gate. But the finding's load-bearing claim — that SC-001a/SC-001b "describe a post-merge/token trigger that does not exist" and are "wrong about the actual gate" — does not withstand scrutiny.

SC-001b (spec.md:68) is misread, not wrong. It says the version appears "after the first token-bearing analysis on main post-merge" and explicitly labels it "post-merge; not a PR-gate criterion... confirmed via the tool against the public read API." It never claims the merge PUSH triggers analysis. "The first token-bearing analysis on main post-merge" is a real, well-defined event: the next nightly cron '17 2 * * *' (or manual workflow_dispatch) on main, which satisfies the :2837 event gate, runs on main, and carries SONAR_TOKEN (a repo secret available to scheduled/dispatch runs, unlike fork PRs). So the trigger exists; SC-001b simply doesn't name the cron. Naming it is a clarity nicety, not a correctness fix, and the finding's "trigger that does not exist" framing is false.

SC-001a (spec.md:67) has only a minor imprecision. Its conclusion — "the sonarcloud job's analysis does not run pre-merge; verified by inspection + a config-assertion, not by a live analysis" — is correct and is the actual acceptance mechanism. Only the parenthetical REASON ("Fork PRs receive no SONAR_TOKEN") is non-operative: the operative pre-merge exclusion is the event gate (per the :2789 comment "PRs skip Sonar entirely"), so even a same-repo PR is excluded despite having the token. That is a genuine but trivial rationale imprecision that changes no acceptance outcome and leaves every criterion verifiable as written.

Net: the finding pairs correct workflow evidence with an overstated read of the spec. It is refuted for SC-001b and reduced for SC-001a to a low-severity cosmetic wording nit in a parenthetical whose conclusion is already right. The recommended rewordings are reasonable clarifications but not a medium drift defect; a verifier following SC-001b's explicit "confirmed via the tool against the public read API" instruction would not be misled into concluding the fix failed. Against the default-to-false bar this does not clearly hold at medium; real=false, with at most a low-severity clarity nit surviving.

dropped.summary: FR-003's core 'do the file sets differ?' question is already answerable from committed config, so the live-API investigation is confirmation, not discovery — bias IC-02 toward the doc outcome.
dropped.why_refuted:
Mechanical evidence is fully accurate (sonar-project.properties:4 sonar.sources=src; :11 exclusions; ci-quality.yml:2704-2715 ten critical_paths globs; --include/--compare-branch/--fail-under=90 at 2738-2741; plan.md IC-02 line 32). But the finding's core inference is a misread and does not withstand scrutiny.

FR-003 has two sub-questions: (a) do the file sets differ? — trivially yes, and config alone shows it; and (b) is there a genuine sources/exclusions MISCONFIGURATION? — the sub-question that actually gates the docs-vs-code-change decision (spec.md:29,41,56; C-002). Sub-question (b) is NOT answerable from `sonar.sources=src` + three exclusion globs: static config states the intended scope, but only enumerating the ACTUAL scored file set via GET /api/measures/component_tree?metricKeys=coverage reveals whether generated/vendored/fixture code is wrongly inflating the coverage denominator. IC-02's live-API call is therefore genuine discovery for the decision-gating question, not mere confirmation of a scope difference nobody disputes. IC-02 is correctly scoped, not over-scoped.

The recommendation is also largely redundant: the plan is already doc-first and already gates any code change on a genuine misconfig — IC-02 line 33 ('Code change only if a genuine sources/exclusions misconfiguration is found — not for a philosophy-only difference'), Key Decisions line 55 ('docs if only threshold philosophy differs; config change only for a genuine file-set misconfig'), Summary line 8 ('one doc'). The finding's ask to 'bias IC-02 toward the doc outcome' describes the plan verbatim.

The finding is additionally self-defeating: its own recommendation still requires the API to run ('unless the API surfaces a genuine exclusion misconfiguration') — i.e. no scope is actually removed. And its extra element — 'pre-commit to the docs outcome' — prejudges sub-question (b), cutting against C-002's research-first discipline and the spec's own framing that coverage 'looks alarmingly low' (spec.md:9), which could be a real exclusion gap; prejudging risks masking it (NFR-002). The 'implementer spins on API spelunking' risk is already bounded to one endpoint/one metric key routed through the purpose-built IC-03 tool. Net: accurate facts, incorrect conclusion; not a genuine over-scope defect.

dropped.summary: IC-03's token-free 'recorded fixture' smoke test has no specified injection seam and zero repo precedent — as scoped it is not achievable
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's factual observations are true but narrow; its load-bearing conclusions are overstated and refuted by the live spec/plan and codebase.

VERIFIED TRUE (but non-load-bearing): scripts/ci/ is indeed Python-only (only quality_gate_decision.py), and no pre-existing curl|jq bash tool is tested offline against a Sonar fixture. The latter is tautological — the tool is authored fresh, so of course no test for it exists yet. The plan also does not name the exact injection seam.

REFUTED — "as scoped it is not achievable" / "zero repo precedent": Testing a curl|jq bash tool offline is a standard technique, and every building block already exists in-repo. The repo has 502 subprocess-based tests, multiple tests that place a fake executable on PATH (the exact seam to stub curl), JSON-fixture patterns (tests/contract/test_handoff_fixtures.py), and many tests that shell out to .sh scripts and assert behavior (tests/architectural/_gate_coverage.py, test_tid251_enforcement.py, test_ci_quality_path_filters.py). The sibling convention the plan cites is real: quality_gate_decision.py IS tested at tests/scripts/test_quality_gate_decision.py. Absence of a test for a not-yet-written tool is not an achievability argument.

REFUTED — "FR-006/NFR-001 (token-free, network-free) cannot be met": (a) The finding conflates network-free with NFR-001. NFR-001 (spec.md:49) requires only NO SONAR_TOKEN — "public read endpoints suffice"; network-freeness is merely a plan Performance Goal (plan.md:17), not a requirement. (b) FR-006 (spec.md:44) explicitly permits the smoke test to run "against the public API (or a recorded fixture)," and the public read API is token-free by the mission's core, independently-verified grounding (spec.md:13, 32, 79). plan.md:37 mirrors this with "plus one optional live-read smoke against the public API." So arg-parsing is testable with zero seam, and output-shape is verifiable against the token-free public API even if no fixture seam is ever built. NFR-001/FR-006 are therefore satisfiable without the seam the finding says is mandatory.

GRANULARITY: Naming the seam (SONAR_API_BASE / --fixture-file / curl stub) is exactly an IC-03 implementation decision; the plan already commits to "arg-parsing + explicit error handling" and a fixture-backed test. Leaving the seam to implementation is normal plan granularity, not a blocking coverage gap.

The only durable kernel is a minor planning-hygiene suggestion (name the seam explicitly), which has mild merit — hence adjusted_severity low — but the finding as written (medium coverage, "not achievable," "cannot be met," "estimate too small") does not withstand adversarial refutation.

Key artifacts: kitty-specs/sonar-qa-config-remediation-01KWYCX7/spec.md (NFR-001 line 49, FR-006 line 44, grounding lines 13/32/79); plan.md (IC-03 lines 35-37, Performance Goals line 17); scripts/ci/quality_gate_decision.py + tests/scripts/test_quality_gate_decision.py (sibling test convention exists).

dropped.summary: Authoring the tool in Bash diverges from the Python-tested scripts/ci/ convention and ships with NO enforced lint gate
dropped.why_refuted: The finding's individual factual claims all verify (scripts/ci/ holds only quality_gate_decision.py, its docstring says "hermetically-testable" at line 4, no shellcheck/super-linter/megalinter/actionlint runs anywhere in .github/ so C-003's "passes shellcheck if the repo runs it" is never enforced, and FR-005 mandates the .sh filename while spec.md:25 calls the maintainer copy "not a source"). But its load-bearing thesis — that Bash is a convention divergence / boundary violation worthy of a medium finding — does not withstand adversarial scrutiny against the live repo. (1) The scripts/ci/ "Python convention" is n=1; a single JSON-decision module does not set a directory-wide language rule. The repo pervasively uses Bash for CI glue: 12 tracked .sh files including a dedicated .github/workflows/scripts/ with six release/CI shell tools, run_tests.sh, scripts/prepare-mutmut-env.sh, scripts/git-hooks/, scripts/tool_configs/. Bash is the established repo idiom, so a curl+jq REST wrapper in Bash conforms rather than diverges. (2) The plan implicitly justifies Bash (plan.md:11-12: "Bash for the review tool" + curl+jq), refuting the "blindly inherited from a filename" claim. (3) "NO enforced lint gate" is the repo-wide baseline for all 12 shell scripts, not a novel gap this mission introduces — no regression or boundary crossing. (4) C-003's no-op is acknowledged by the spec author's own conditional wording ("if the repo runs it"), not a concealed defect. (5) The recommendation over-sells Python: the plan already handles the test seam via a recorded fixture + pytest driver (plan.md:37), which is language-agnostic, so Python is not required to remove the "fixture seam." What survives is at most a LOW-severity plan-wording optimism (plan.md:14/36 mention "shellcheck-clean" as though a gate exists), already hedged by C-003's conditional — insufficient to sustain the finding as framed. Defaulting to real=false per the standard, since the medium boundary framing is clearly refuted by live artifacts.

dropped.summary: IC-02's comparison basis is muddled: the internal diff-coverage gate has no static 'file set' to compare against Sonar's whole-repo coverage
dropped.why_refuted:
Evidence is quoted correctly but the interpretation is a misread; IC-02 already contains the distinction the finding claims is missing, and the alleged risk is fully guarded.

FACTS VERIFIED (live repo): ci-quality.yml:2704-2715 defines a static committed `critical_paths=( 'src/kernel/*' 'src/doctrine/*' … )` glob list; 2738-2741 runs `diff-cover … --fail-under=90 --include "${critical_paths[@]}"` against `origin/${base_ref}` (per-PR-diff, filtered to critical paths). sonar-project.properties:4 is `sonar.sources=src` (whole-repo), :11 `sonar.exclusions`. Quoting is accurate.

WHY NOT REAL:
1) "The internal gate has no static file set" is factually wrong. `critical_paths` (2704-2715) IS a static, committed glob list defining an enumerable file set; IC-02 correctly names it the gate's "critical-path file list."
2) IC-02 is not "muddled." plan.md:32 and spec.md:29 already define the threshold philosophy as "(whole-repo average vs per-PR-diff-only)" and instruct the investigator to distinguish a file-set difference from a philosophy difference — exactly the reframe the finding recommends. The finding's recommendation is substantively already the plan's design.
3) The alleged "false misconfiguration verdict / spurious config-bug conclusion" is blocked by four layers: IC-02's own "Code change only if a genuine sources/exclusions misconfiguration is found — not for a philosophy-only difference"; spec.md:29's triple gate ("materially misconfigured" + "only if it is a genuine config bug" + "Do not force a code change where docs are the honest answer"); C-002 (HIGH: "a philosophy-only difference is discharged by documentation"); and NFR-002/SC-004 (no suppression/ratchet, independently forbidding a sonar.sources narrowing). The critical-path subset is, per the YAML's own comment ("Coverage effort is proportional to risk"), an intentional design choice — not a sources/exclusions misconfig; Sonar should score all of src. So even under the finding's most charitable "both differ" reading, the plan's outcome is document-don't-fix, which is what the finding wants.
4) The recommended alternative (compare sonar.sources/exclusions to coverage.xml contents) is narrower than and inferior to IC-02's live component_tree enumeration and de-emphasizes the file-set comparison US2 actually needs.
5) Re-litigation: .gates/post-spec.json (lines 62, 73-74, 82) already refuted three same-family findings (incl. an explicit "false dichotomy … file sets AND philosophy both differ" one) as real=false, with anchored reasoning. No new evidence is presented here.

Net: accurate code quote, misread plan; the distinction and the research-first guardrails the finding claims are absent are already present and binding. If entertained at all it is at most an informational wording nicety (acknowledge file-set and philosophy differences co-occur), not an outcome-changing defect — hence not real, and no upward severity.
